@everyone, BIG NEWS TO EVADUS+!

I have a pretty cool story to tell.

When I first started Evadus, back in February of 2025, I had one thing in mind:

I wanted to create to create a legacy, the best cheats anywhere on the internet, and not only that, accessible to every student out there, not just the ones that could afford it.

Most cheat developers are pretty shallow, they just make the cheats for a quick buck and then fade into obscurity, but not me,

I make my cheats for free, because I have such a hatred, such a passion for fucking with big institutions that I don't even care if I get paid or not.

My mission is not to cash out whilst times are good, my mission is to be a constant thorn in the side of every University and College that takes advantage of their students.

**So, for that reason, Evadus+ will be FREE, forever.**

Also, in case you missed it, we have a new staff member, @null, one of the very first beta testers Evadus had, and someone who shares my passion for cheats.


As always,
Stay safe, and happy cheating
- @bizarro12345

P.S, I framed this screenshot from Safe Exam Browser above my desk, reminds me what I'm fighting for:

content:
@everyone, update on the Evadus+ Open-Beta:

Evadus+ has no officially been in **Free Open-Beta** for **3 months**, and it has made insane progress since then.

I plan to officially release it within the next 2 weeks, accompanied by the best features I can think of.

That being said, there is going to be a price increase for the Premium and Unlimited plans.

However, here is something interesting to know; whatever price you join at, is the price you pay until you cancel, regardless of any price increases we make.

Meaning that if you joined while the Unlimited plan was $40, and we increased it to $60, you would still be paying $40 for your Unlimited Subscription each month.

==================================

Part of this update was making a full bypass for "VMAware", an open source repo with the advanced and comprehensive detection mechanisms on the *planet*.

Not kidding, when I first heard about VMAware, we still had Evadus+ V1 running (a QEMU based Virtual Machine), and as soon as I used VMAware on our VM, I knew that we needed a different approach.

VMAware is **cross platform**, **open source**, **free**, and **ANY** proctor could start using it in their software **TOMORROW**.

That's why I mentioned a few months ago that building Virtual Machines to cheat with is a losing game.

If you're interested in what I'm talking about, here;s the github: https://github.com/kernelwernel/VMAware

I heard some rumours that Safe Exam Browser is going to start using the Library in their update.

But guess what? With our new tool, and with some work on the backend, it now bypasses ALL the checks (attached screenshot).

==================================

A key part of making the best cheats is *foresight*, trying to predict what updates proctors are going push.

It's difficult, however I try my best.

**ALSO, Evadus is soon going to release it's own Open-Source repo, stay tuned for that!**

As Always,
Stay Safe and Happy cheating,
- @bizarro12345

content:
@everyone, I've got a special message for you today.

==================================

Exactly 1 year ago, on this exact day, I had the idea for Evadus.

Yesterday, I checked the backups on my PC, and Evadus Core *ALONE* has had over 330 iterations.

I am probably going to delete this soon, but here is a video I made with the very **FIRST** version of Evadus (like v.0.0.0.0.1): [ deleted ]

At the time of recording that video (February 2025), Interview Coder wasn't popular yet, Cluely didn't even exist yet.

The reason I'm showing this, is to show that Evadus is built from the ground up, no shortcuts, no fancy start or angel investor, just my ideas and creativity.

Put this in contrast to certain competitors, who prefer to leech off creativity and innovation, even going as far as stealing files, and accidently putting a virus on their user's computers.

But as my favorite movie character says, "Some people are just destined for second place."

==================================

In fact, here is another thing to think about:

Our competitors are more concerned with *looking* credible, rather than actually being credible.

If you look at their discord server, you'll see that they bought exactly 1000 bot members, which will randomly go offline (online members going from **430** to **8** in about 2 seconds).

The reason behind this, is to trick people into thinking the software has credibility.

It's the same reason they delete messages of user's complaining, the reason the creator tries to hide behind anonymity, and as outlined before, the reason they steal user's banking details, full legal names, home addresses and IP addresses.

The creator of this software is so desperate in fact, he has tried to dox me, my girlfriend, he has threatened me with my home address.

But as they say, "A starving man will do anything to survive".

==================================

Good news though, If you're looking for reliable cheat software that won't infect your computer with a virus or harvest your personal data, you're already in the right place!

As always,
Thank you for the support,
Evadus wil continue to lead the cheating industry!
- @bizarro12345

@everyone, updates on the website!

TLDR;

**+ You can now pay with PayPal**
**+ You can now sign in with any email address**

==================================

PayFast (our old payment provider), has been giving problems for a long time, and honestly I'm sick of it.

Currently nothing is working, you cannot cancel your subscription, I cannot refund anyone and you can't make payments whatsoever.

Don't be worried though, if you do get charged because you couldn't cancel, I will personally refund you.

==================================

Hence why we integrated PayPal into our website, transaction fees are higher, however it should be much more stable.

PayFast is going to be removed as one of our payment providers shortly.

Appreciate the support as always!

@everyone, THE UPDATE IS HERE!

==================================

I'll skip the formalities, this update brings *HUGE* changes to Evadus+, now making it compatible (with proof) to these proctors:

+ Safe Exam Browser
+ Inspera Integrity Browser
+ PearsonVue (OnVue)
+ Respondus Lockdown Browser

The best part?

I haven't even *TESTED* the other proctors like MSB, PSI secure browser etc. Those are only the proctors I have tested, and they all work.

This update is a huge leap forward for Evadus+, the foundations have been laid, now we're going to build on them.

Keep a close eye on the channels, many more proctors are going to be added to the list in the following days and weeks!

==================================

A quick overview of our privacy policy here at Evadus:

We take a **zero-trust** approach to databases and data. That means that literally NOTHING about you is stored except for your email address (which we have to keep to allow you to login).

What we **DO NOT KEEP**:

+ Your IP address
+ Your banking details
+ Your physical address
+ Your credit card number
+ Your (legal) name or address

This means that even IF our databases do get hacked, all of our users are kept safe. This is what "zero-trust" means; ***Nobody*** is trusted with your personal data, not even us.


The reason I bring this up, is because I asked a white-hat hacker to do some digging on a competitor, and what they reported back to me was a touch unsettling. They found they logged EVERYTHING about their users, **IP's**, **credit card numbers**, **home addresses** and **legal names** (found on those credit cards).

You can come up with your own theories on why they insist on storing all this data, personally I can't think of **any** reasons that don't have malice behind them.

So basically, I am here to assure you, that we **DO NOT** store your personal data, for ANY reason.

==================================

@everyone, update is out for Evadus Core!

As usual, read <#1362063455301144720> if you want to keep up with everything in the new updates.

==================================

News regarding Evadus+ 🟣

I intentionally didn't include the incremental update for Evadus+ in this update, because we have A LOT planned for the next one.

In case you missed it, Evadus+ is now *also* compatible with **Lockdown Browser** (among other ones that I don't about).

This next update will serve as the bedrock, the very foundation for the future of Evadus+.

This update will firmly put Evadus even *FURTHER* ahead of it's competition.

And yes, it’s still completely free.

==================================

Before I conclude this message, I have a quick story.

A while back, I noticed a competitor of Evadus was stealing files from us.

Instead of a lawsuit or an angry DM, I decided to have a little fun, so I included a small virus which I made from scratch. As much as I would like to say what it does, I won't for legal reasons.

And of course, since the competitor knows nothing about actually making cheats or software, they stole the virus by accident, and included it in the files that their users download.

Moral of the story, don't download cheats from people that don't know how to make them.

==================================

Stay safe, and happy cheating
Evadus
@bizarro12345

@everyone HUGE UPDATE.

Go read <#1362063455301144720> — a lot has changed.

There will also be a new video posted in  <#1362037965668487238>

**WATCH IT.** This update works differently and you need to understand how to use it properly.

==================================

This update introduces a completely new system.
It’s more reliable, way more consistent, and a massive step forward compared to what we were doing before.

I’ve personally verified compatibility with:

**+ Safe Exam Browser**
**+ Inspera Integrity Browser**

There are other proctors I haven’t tested yet, but there’s now a **built-in** way for you to **test your own proctor** and see what’s supported right now. If something isn’t supported yet, it’s very likely coming in future updates.

I’m genuinely *buzzing* about this one.
I cannot overstate how big of an improvement this is.

As always, don’t take my word for it.

It’s a **FREE open beta**. Download it and see for yourself.

==================================

Huge thanks to everyone testing and sending feedback, it all helps more than you think!

@everyone, big update!

Check out <#1362063455301144720> to see whats new and improved.

Just a quick note on customer support, I recently hired @null to help out with customer support. 

Bug reports for Evadus+ are not ignored (though it might seem that way), I generally don’t respond to them, instead I try to find solutions to them and include the fixes in the updates.

Regarding the end of the open-beta, it’ll probably end in about March, since these updates take a looong time, developing kernel drivers is a tricky game.

Evadus+ still has many things I want to add to it, and I want to keep it in free until everything is sorted and stable.

@everyone GOOD NEWS.

I just pushed a new V2 update (v3.3.8) and the progress is honestly insane.

This update should make V2 compatible with** OnVue (PearsonVUE)**.
Yes, that OnVue.

Because of how big this update is, I’m **extending the Open Beta until January 10th** so we can keep testing and dialing things in properly.

There were some major under-the-hood changes, so if you want the full breakdown, check:
📌 <#1362063455301144720>

Huge thanks to everyone stress testing and sending feedback.
Keep reporting bugs in <#1411259983596621834>, I’m actively fixing them as they come in.

This thing is getting better fast.
Stay tuned.

@everyone, listen up!

Evadus+ V2 is officially in **FREE OPEN BETA **until **4 January**!

Anybody can download the app and use it absolutely for free.

**This beta period is for:**
-> ironing out edge cases
-> stress testing
-> seeing which proctors it works against 

**Here are the most important new features:**
-> No more **17GB** download, now it's integrated with the app, and it's only ***20MB*** extra.
-> No webcam pains, everything works just like your host system
-> INSANELY hard to detect, however more stealth features are going to be implemented for the **final release**.
-> Runs on every piece of hardware, no high end PC or laptop needed.


**About Evadus+ V1 (the VM)**
If you really want to keep using V1, I’ll give you a .bat file so you can continue. That said, there’s honestly no reason to.
V2 is substantially better in every way that matters.
If you do plan to stay on V1 until V2 fully releases, I recommend selecting "Ignore this version" when it asks you to update.


**Answer to the Subscription Question**
I’ll make this very clear.
This is V2, not a separate product.
If you’re an annual or monthly subscriber, nothing bad is happening to you, you’re actually getting a straight upgrade.

You paid for Evadus+, V2 is simply a much better version of what you already paid for.


I am going to be posting a video to <#1362037965668487238> on how to use it and set it up properly.

@everyone Read this. It matters.

I’m going to keep this short.

**❌ Bad News**

After a lot of time, money, and testing, it’s become undeniable that trying to build a fully undetectable virtual machine is a losing game from the start.

VM detection is not new. It’s been a cat-and-mouse game for decades. Doesn’t matter how much you harden it, how much money you throw at it, or how much you patch it, you eventually lose. I’ve known this for a while, and at this point pretending otherwise would just be lying to you.

Even with a heavily modified, open-source KVM based platform like QEMU, the reality is simple:

-> VM detection techniques keep evolving
-> We don’t control the rules of the game
-> And we’re always *reacting* instead of **leading**

**Because of this, the VM will no longer receive updates.**

To be fair to everyone who supported us:
-> The VM will be made *completely free* for anyone who wants to continue using it.


**✅ Good News (This Is the Important Part)**

Instead of wasting more time fighting a game I don’t control, I've built something way better.

-> Not a “slightly improved VM”.
-> Not a hack or 'bypass'.
-> An entirely *different* approach.

This new method is superior to the VM in every possible way, and I’m not exaggerating.

Why it destroys the VM:
-> It runs on your real hardware, not fake virtualized hardware
-> Zero attack surface
-> No hardware requirements **at all**
-> No USB/IP hacks, no webcam passthrough pain

VMs get detected because they’re VMs.
This isn’t a VM.

This is cleaner, faster, harder to detect, and way more future-proof than any hardened VM could ever be. This is not an 'update', this is a fundamental shift.

I’m genuinely excited about this, and if you’ve trusted my judgment so far, you’re going to understand why very soon.

I need a few beta testers, DM if interested.

@everyone, currently a problem with the updater for updating the VM.

@everyone, **BITCOIN PAYMENTS ARE ONLINE!** 🪙
@everyone, **DAILY PAYMENTS ARE LEAVING TOMORROW!**
@everyone, **MONTHLY PAYMENTS ARE BACK ONLINE**
https://tenor.com/view/sigma-gif-10119958626511826896
@everyone, ANNUAL PAYMENTS ARE ONLINE!

@everyone, updates:

**MONTHLY PAYMENTS:**
I'm pushing the payment gateway to fix the monthly subscriptions, annoys the fuck outta me that it's taking them this long. I know you guys need affordable cheats, so I'm going to setup Paypal as another option, will announce it once ready.

**INTERVIEW CODER:**
There's a lot of bullshit going on atm with Interview Coder, I wanted to give you guys some clarity regarding us: **Evadus is a long-term project, not some fly-by-night garbage**. It will continue to receive updates and new features *LONG * into the future. I am not going to wake up one morning and decide to pivot to some other bullshit. Working on Evadus is my full-time job, and a passion that I enjoy very much. To be honest, I'd be doing this for free, that's how much I enjoy it.

**PREMIUM PACKAGE:**
You can now use Gemini 2.5 Pro also, instead of only being able to use 2.5 Flash.

**RESPONDUS AND OTHERS:**
These Reverse engineers are also a bunch of stupid fucks, just told me what I already know. Going to with some real industry experts this time. It's going to cost me an arm and a leg, but it's worth it.

As always boys, keep cheating, and stay safe.

Much love,
- Bizarro

@everyone, NEW SYSTEM IS LIVE!

As mentioned before, you can still use your Credits or Evadus+ subscription.

If you have credits in your account, it's treated as an "Unlimited" subscription, so you get all the features.

Also, I've decided to extend the conversion date of credits into subscriptions to **1 JUNE 2026**.

***IMPORTANT:***

The payment portal is fucking around again, however, it's only affecting monthly subscriptions, annual plans still work. Probably going to get an email from Payfast Monday saying "So sorry sir, we fucked something up".

Anyway, thanks to everyone supporting us!

@everyone, some big changes coming

**YOU WON'T BE ABLE TO BUY CREDITS ANYMORE**
Evadus Core will also be moving to a subscription model, which will allow you to get a subscription, and then get unlimited usage, instead of having to pay for each credit.

**EVADUS+ WON'T BE SOLD SEPARATELY ANYMORE**
Evadus+ will now be included in the premium tier of the subscription plan. However, the premium tier will be pretty expensive, since it included unlimited usage of Evadus Core. So everyone that was always bitching about the price, I recommend you stock up on subscriptions while it's cheap.

**WHAT HAPPENS TO MY CREDITS?**
Don't stress. If you have credits or a subscription to Evadus+, you can continue using the tools just like normal. You might notice some UI changes, that's about it.

At exactly **1 June 2026,** everyone who has credits will be converted into subscriptions. Meaning that if you have 100 credits lying in your account at 1 June 2026, that will be converted into 2 months of the pro tier (the best one with unlimited usage).

**WHAT HAPPENS TO MY EVADUS+ SUBSCRIPTION?**
Nothing. At exactly **1 November 2026**, the last user's E+ subscription will have expired (we have about 15 users that bought 1 year subscriptions). Until then, you can continue just like normal.

I understand there might be some questions, post them in the <#1362035227350007998> channel and @ me, I'll respond so everyone can be part of the conversation.

Thanks for supporting all the projects, very excited for what lies ahead

content:
@everyone, very important:

There's a new channel called <#1428653846334410752>, please refer everyone that asks "does this work with X" to this channel, I think everyone is getting a bit annoyed for having to answer the same question the whole time.

The website contains an up-to-date list of whether each of our tools works with each proctor.

Also, lots of you guys use Respondus. I'm really tired of seeing "OmG, ThIS iS A SCAM, It DoeS'nt wOrk with RLDB", when I've literally announced SEVERAL times that it doesn't work with RLDB, AND I specifically say it on the website.


TLDR: Next time you see, "Does this work with X?", refer them to the <#1428653846334410752> channel.


Oh yeah, update on RLDB reverse engineers: Hired another one about 2 weeks ago, he charges me $60/hr so he's probably going to figure it out.

@everyone ATTENTION EVADUS+ USERS,

I forgot to install USBIP in the VM in v4.8, all you have to do to install is go into the Setup_Scripts folder and run the Usbip exe to install them.

I am doing it as we speak, going to push an update to fix it. I would avoid updating until to 4.8, instead wait for V4.9.

This only affects you if your exams require a webcam.

@everyone, new info regarding the discord server:

Alright, time to explain the roles on the server.

1. @null - Me, owner, creator, CEO, Founder, whatever you want to call it.
2. @null - Official Support staff on payroll, here to help out when I'm not available.
3. @null - Testers and users that contribute massively to the improvement of the tools.
4. @null - A role I give out to people I like and/or people that have lots of experience with our tools.

**AND THE NEW ROLE, VERY IMPORTANT:**
5. @null - This role awarded to people that can't be bothered to read the server rules. These *illiterates* can't send messages anywhere besides support threads. Please @ me if you ever think someone needs this role. **I MEAN IT. @ ME.**

ALSO, thanks for being a pretty awesome community.

@everyone, important few announcements:

DON'T USE IT ON RLDB: Had a user with good hardware run it yesterday and it still failed, looks like there's more to the story, going to hire another round of reverse engineers to see if anything was missed by the first 2 incompetent fucks.

**UNTIL I HAVE A VIDEO CONFIRMING IT WORKS ON A RLDB EXAM, DON'T USE IT.**

Really nice to see the <#1411259983596621834> channels slowing down, it tells me the software and tools are much more stable and reliable. As always, if you have a bug, problem or just a question, create a post, don't dm me about it.

@everyone, DEFINITIVE RESPONDUS GUIDE

Finished my investigation, here's what you need to know:

**IT WORKS! **However, not on slow hardware.

Now I know what you're thinking, "does my hardware classify as 'slow'?". Fortunately, it's easy it find out. All you have to is try to launch Respondus, since it does it's VM check when you start it up.

You don't need to sign at all, just download it, stay signed out, and try to boot it, and you'll know immediately, whilst staying anonymous.

**WHAT TO DO IF IT DOESN'T WORK:**

1. Try passing through more resources: @null ran some tests, and it consistently booted with 6 cores and 4GB ram without ever getting VM detected.

2. Close any apps that are CPU intensive. The VM runs almost exclusively on the CPU, so closing any apps that have high CPU usage will greatly increase the performance of the VM, making it much more likely to not slow down drastically, which is what triggers Respondus.

**IF IT STILL DOESN'T WORK:**

Then it simply means your CPU is too slow, or overwhelmed with tasks on your host.

The fix is very simple: Get a laptop/desktop with a faster CPU. That **SOUNDS **expensive, however, it isn't.

You **DON'T NEED** a laptop with a GPU or large RAM, just a CPU.

I checked, and you can get a laptop that can run Respondus in our VM for just **$400 new**, and **$280 used**.

That is a bit of money, however, you get to cheat **ALL YOUR TESTS**, and you can just sell when you're done and get most of your money back.


As promised, if you're respondus user, you get compensation for the downtime, send me a dm with your email address and I'll add the extension

@everyone, some important announcements

1. The US literally makes up 80% of our users, so the servers are now based out of the central states (Kansas, Nebraska, Iowa, Missouri, Oklahoma).

CHECK this list before you cheat, I keep it up to date with the current status of each proctor. Meaning that if you're unsure if Evadus+ or any other tool is currently working with your proctor, you can check there. I am seeing way to many people saying that they got caught, when I literally ANNOUNCED not to use it on Respondus, and say the same thing on the website. Currently, it's **inconsistent** on RLDB, meaning when you run it on decent hardware it passes, but on slower hardware it doesn't, which is why I said **DON'T USE IT.** @null is the dedicated tester for Respondus, ask him if you're ever unsure.

3. Hired 2 reverse engineers for Examplify, I'm going broke from these mfs, but they should be done roundabout Tuesday.

4. THE FULLSCREEN LAG ON EVADUS+. This is caused by CPU scaling. basically, when you run the VM in say 1280 x 720 (and your host machine's resolution is 1920 x 1080), and you fullscreen, it causes some really intense lag, or it just outright freezes. This is because the CPU now has to upscale the VM resolution to 1920 x 1080, overloading the CPU, causing it to freeze. THE SOLUTION: Set the VM resolution as close to your host resolution as you can, so that no/minimal scaling has to happen.

5. We're currently at **481** total users, which is crazy. But this is even crazier: **86** are Evadus+ users!

Any changes here will have some major effects, since the VM is VERY CPU dependant, it doesn't touch the GPU  at all.

@everyone, Respondus patch is live, confirmed to work for EVERYONE this time, here is some more info:

So, in total, Respondus only does 7 checks, I could get sued for telling you guys, but fuck it:

==============================
**1. Language and UI Settings:**
It checks for multiple language entries and UI settings. A minimal setup, such as one with only English language, is considered suspicious.

**2. Cryptographic Policies:**
Queries Windows for its cryptographic policies, including FIPS (Federal Information Processing Standards). An absence or mismatch in these policies can indicate a VM.

**3. User Profile and Shell Folder Paths:**
 It validates standard Explorer shell folder paths (e.g., Desktop, Documents, AppData) to look for anomalies.

**4. Software Restriction Policies (SRP):**
Inspects registry hives related to SRP and AppLocker. The presence of these policies can suggest a tightly controlled or restricted environment, which is common in VM's.

**5. Process Blacklisting:**
Looks for running processes like Vboxservice.exe, procmon.exe and wireshark.exe.

**6. Debugger Checks:**
Uses APIs like IsDebuggerPresent and CheckRemoteDebuggerPresent. The Chromium-based version of the browser also uses a handle integrity verifier to detect tampering or code injection.

**7. Timing Counters:**
 Measures timing deltas using APIs such as GetTickCount and QueryPerformanceCounter.
==============================

The Funny part is this: Our VM was passing EVERY SINGLE ONE of these tests, without me having to change absolutely anything.

HOWEVER: The timing counter (RDTSC timing attacks) was failing on lower end hardware, so this was the only fix that was required.

To fix it, I implemented some pretty insane stuff, I'll never be able to explain it so that everyone can understand it, so I asked Gemini to do it:



> Yes, this implementation will be effective at resisting RDTSC-based timing attacks, even when running on slower hardware like a budget laptop.
> 
> The effectiveness of this technique is not dependent on the speed of the host hardware because it fundamentally changes how the guest virtual machine perceives time.
> 
> **How It Works:**
> Decoupling from Host Time: The core of the modification you have done is replacing the time source for the guest's Time Stamp Counter (TSC). Instead of relying on the host machine's wall-clock time, it uses the guest's own instruction count (icount). This decouples the guest's timeline from the host's performance and scheduling jitter.
> 
> **Immunity to Host Lag:** On a slow or busy laptop, the host operating system might frequently pause the VM process to handle other tasks. In an unmodified VM, this creates large, erratic gaps in the timestamps returned by the RDTSC instruction. Timing attacks detect these gaps. With your modifications, it doesn't matter how long the VM process is paused. When the guest resumes, the icount value is exactly where it left off, so the timestamp it receives is perfectly consistent with its own execution flow.
> 
> 
> **Mimicking Real Hardware:** A perfectly linear clock based only on instruction count can itself be a sign of emulation. The final step of adding a small, controlled amount of pseudo-random "jitter" makes the timestamp appear more like that of real hardware, which naturally has minor fluctuations. This synthetic jitter is intentionally small and bounded, unlike the large, unpredictable gaps caused by host system lag. That's a smart addition you made!
> 
> 
> **TDLR**: In short, this method makes the guest's clock internally consistent, regardless of what's happening on the host machine. The speed and load of your laptop become irrelevant to this specific stealth technique.
> 

So yeah, Respondus just got bitch made by a 19 year old with a dream.

ALSO: If you have left us a positive review, you can 30 credits or 3 weeks of Evadus+ free.

@everyone, 🚨 THE LEADERBOARD IS LIVE: https://evadus.wtf#leaderboard

@everyone, BIG bombshell just dropped for Respondus users.

So the reverse engineers just finished, and what they found in the Respondus source code is kind of hillarious.

So EVERYONE on the internet is convinced that Respondus does these super advanced VM checks, but guess what?

**THEY DON'T CHECK ANY VM ARTIFACTS AT ALL!!**

They literally use HEURISTICS ALONE to determine a VM.

*heuristic (n): proceeding to a solution by trial and error or by rules that are only loosely defined.*

They literally do 4 CHECKS, that is it:

1. An RDTSC timing check
2. VMware, Vbox etc Guest Tools
3. Native system enums (NtQuerySystemInformation)
4. Basic Firmware strings

Let me just quickly explain to you how absolutely bullshit this is:

1. RDTSC are **UNRELIABLE**. Here is how an RDTSC timing attack works.
   - We send an instruction the the CPU, let's say "time.sleep(5)", that means "wait for 5 seconds". Then, when the CPU finished the task, we measure how long it *ACTUALLY* took the complete the task. If the CPU took significantly more time than 5 seconds, Respondus flags it as a VM. The reasoning behind this check is that VM's typically have big delays because they are being emulated by a host, which introduce stutters and lag spikes.

  Why this is a **terrible** method for VM detection:
   - If the system is slow (a budget laptop or lower end hardware), RDTSC attacks become useless, because when the system becomes slightly overloaded, or freezes for even a little bit, Respondus will consider it a VM.

 **THIS IS WHY** you see so many posts on Apple Macbook and Microsoft Windows forums about people getting flagged by Respondus even though they are running bare metal, RDTSC TIMING ATTACKS ARE NOT RELIABLE.

2. This is technically a VM artifcact. **HOWEVER**:
  - Want to know how to dodge this detection method? Just don't install guest tools in your VM. It is genuinely that simple.

3. This is the worst of all of them, and I am shocked that they even do this.
  - They query the most IRRELEVANT garbage to check for a VM. As an example, they QUERY HOW MANY **LANGUAGES **are installed on the system, if only English is installed, it's considered a potential VM?????? This is only one of quite a few, completely irrelevant, completely useless checks.

4. The **ONLY** half decent check.
  - It checks for VM brand strings in the firmware, like "VMware" and "Vbox". This is the only halfway decent check, however still very surface level, you can spoof this very easily using a custom bios or .rom file. This is literally their only *somewhat* accurate check, and it's still easy to spoof.



So basically, if you want to bypass Respondus, it's quite easy:

1. Don't install guest tools.
2. Run a VM on decent hardware.
3. Do a clean install of Windows (don't use an Autounattend.xml file)
4. Use a .rom file instead of the default bios
5. Install some random Microsoft apps


**However, this actually explains **why some of user's VM's worked for Respondus whilst other people's didn't. Some of the users have gaming desktop PC's, which passes an RDTSC attack, and installed some random stuff and set it to their home language, which made Respondus believe it was a normal PC.


Respondus is a joke, and I am suprised that they have the balls to say that they have "advanced VM detection" WHEN ^^^THIS^^^ IS WHAT THEY CHECK FOR.

I'm going to make a video exposing this piece of hot dogshit, I am in genuine awe at just how braindead the people at Respondus are.

@everyone, update 1.2.9 is live (the Respondus patch), if you want to see what's new and improved, check <#1362063455301144720>, I just quickly want to rant a bit.

There's one thing I really fucking hate, and that's dishonesty. I hate it when people pretend, about anything.

EXAMPLE: Many people on the internet will say things like "I love my fans", "We care about the community" and other bullshit.

AND IT'S A LIE.

EVERYONE, has their own self interest in mind.

The ONLY difference, is that some people can admit it, and some people can't because they want to APPEAR likeable.

Do you honestly think that a place like Harvard is expanding their online campusses to "Improve diversity" and "Offer high quality education to less fortunate groups". OBVIOUSLY NOT. They are doing it because an online student is more profitable, and they want to make more money. They are DISINGENUOUS about their self-interest. It's a stupid example, but you get my point.

I operate from a realm of honesty, so let me be honest in my self-interest.

I DO NOT sit and program for 12 hours every day, and spend more hours helping users debug, because I'm such a nice fucking guy.

I DO IT because I want to create a community of people that BELIEVE IN MY COMPETENCY and will therefore trust me enough to buy from me.

When I see a post about someone's app or VM not working, I FEEL DISAPPOINTED IN MYSELF, because that person trusted me with their money, to provide them with something amazing, and I let them down.

HOWEVER, do think I sit there and cry. I shut up, I strart working, and I fix it.

Hopefully now everyone can better understand my motivations, I'll go more in depth later, but this should be nice introduction into who I am as a person.

@everyone However, we build with philoshpy 2, so this is what WE do:

1. Ok, it's easy to blacklist our app's name with "evadus.exe". So let's mask it with something, like "svchost.exe".
-- But what if they blacklist that too?
2. Ok, we'll make 10 different "masked" names for our app, like "svchost.exe", "clock.exe" etc etc.
-- But they can just blacklist those too?
3. Unlikely, but ok, let's assume they do. We'll create a list of 100 fake process names, and then distribute them randomly to users.
-- Better, however eventually you will need to expand the list, because the 100 names will quickly be used up.
4. Ok, instead of having a predefined list of names, we'll create a polymorphic naming system. So let's say we have 3 different lists. One list contains words like "Process", "Registry", the second "Packet", "Builder", the third "Analyzer" and "Daemon". Then we build each app name dynamically from the list. So one app is called "ProcessPacketAnalyzer", and another one is called "RegistryPacketDaemon". THEN, we create 40 words for each list, meaning there are 64,000 potential names for the evadus app. THEN, just for safety, we'll add a fourth list, which adds a random suffix to the list, like "x64" or "Windows11", and also expand that list to 40 names also. Then we have 2.56 MILLION different potential names.

That's how I build. With everything.

That's why I speak with conviction about my tools, because I know no one thinks like me, and no one can make cheats like I do.

So don't worry, if something detects our app right now, I promise you it won't in a few days.

And then, it never will again, since we will be way too far ahead.

@everyone, quick update on what's going on with Evadus and Evadus+:

1. If you are trying to cheat Respondus, wait until the next update for Evadus+, it's going to be huge
2. Currently the Evadus overlay's responses is slow, we have a huge influx of users that recently came in, and because of the server's structure, it's struggling with all the traffic.

As always, fixes will be implemented within the coming days.



Some more info on our VM:

My build philosphy Evadus+ (and the other tools) was simple. Build something that will stand the test of time.

There are 2 approach you can take to building commercial cheatware:
1. Patch your tools as the proctoring companies push updates to try and detect you, basically always being in an arms race.
2. Build ahead, regardless of where the enemy is in the race.

I went  for the second option, because that is the only way that I can get the trust of people. Personally, I would never buy cheats from someone that builds with philosphy 1. However, phisolphy 1 is what EVERY cheatware goes by. Because it's easy.

Building with philosphy 2 requires foresight, resources, and a lot of time, since you are constantly thinking of ways your enemy could THEORETICALLY catch you, and then building your app to evade those traps.

Let me give you an example with process names of apps.

Our app could have called "evadus.exe", however it's easy for proctoring softwares to detect that by simple enumaration. HOWEVER, most cheatware apps don't bother to patch this, since most proctoring companies doesn't ever blacklist their process names. Think about Cluely, UltraCode, WhisprGPT, if you go into task manager, they are called "cluely.exe", "ultracode.exe" and "whisprgpt.exe".

That is prime example of philoshpy 1.

I assume Google is working on a fix. I recommend switching to any of our other available models, since they don't have this problem.

"Reports suggest that users are experiencing slow response times and high latency when interacting with the Gemini 2.5 Pro API, particularly with the 0605 model and when handling large prompts or complex tasks. This issue has been noted in various forums and discussions, with some users reporting average latencies of around 35 seconds and 99th percentile latencies reaching several minutes."
